mnemonics: P.I.P.E.S: Parallel Input/Output: Partition Everything Sensibly
keyPoints: Use parallel file systems (e.g., Lustre, GPFS) designed for concurrent access from multiple nodes.
Implement collective I/O operations where processes coordinate to read/write large contiguous blocks instead of many small, non-contiguous requests.
Stripe files across multiple physical disks to aggregate bandwidth and allow simultaneous access.
commonMistakes: Allowing every process to perform independent, small I/O operations, leading to a 'I/O storm' that overwhelms the storage system.
Not considering the underlying storage architecture (e.g., network vs. local disk) when designing the I/O strategy, resulting in unexpected bottlenecks.
